headline: Tightening Credit Conditions and Evolving Regulatory Tailwinds Shaping Analyst Narrative Around JPMorgan Chase (JPM)
summary: JPMorgan Chase & Co. (NYSE:JPM) is included in our list of the best stocks to buy for financial stability. As of March 24, 2026, JPMorgan Chase & Co. (NYSE:JPM) retains the confidence of roughly 70% of covering analysts, who maintain bullish ratings on the stock. The $350 consensus price target implies over 20% upside. Tightening […]

headline: Inside ‘Project Eagle,’ JPMorgan’s High-Wire Act to Fund EA Deal
summary: (Bloomberg) -- The post from @realDonaldTrump landed at precisely 7:23 a.m. Inside JPMorgan Chase & Co., the final piece of Project Eagle was a go.For weeks, JPMorgan bankers had anxiously watched events in the Middle East as they worked to sell the financing for the biggest leveraged buyout ever, the $55-billion private equity takeover of video-game giant Electronic Arts Inc. headline: AI Schism Grips Washington as Tech, Labor Vie for Upper Hand
summary: (Bloomberg) -- At a historic auditorium in Washington this week, Silicon Valley executives, Trump administration officials and members of Congress gathered in a sea of button-down shirts to extol the virtues of artificial intelligence.Two days later, labor leaders joined a handful of US lawmakers in a DC hotel ballroom to strategize how to fight back as AI threatens to displace workers and remake the global economy.The dueling events – one funded by top tech companies including OpenAI and Alphab

headline: Dip-buyers arrive to pull gold back from brink of a bear market
summary: (Bloomberg) -- Opportunistic buyers are starting to emerge in the gold market after the biggest selloff in years, helping to keep bullion’s record-breaking three-year bull run intact.Gold prices have fallen by 15% this month, creating a test of faith for gold bulls. That’s left some questioning the precious metal’s safe-haven credentials as the Iran war threatens to upend the global economy. The meltdown has come as a broader selloff across stocks, bonds and currencies prompted investors to sell

headline: How The Standard Chartered (LSE:STAN) Story Is Shifting As Analyst Targets Converge
summary: Standard Chartered’s central fair value estimate is now set at £19.25 versus £18.94 previously, with fresh analyst price targets clustering between £18.65 and £22.00 around that mark. Taken together, the lifts to £17.75, £18.30 and £22.00, alongside the trim to £18.65 from £21.22, show that recent research is constructive overall but not unanimous on how much upside the shares might justify. As you read on, you will see how to interpret these shifting targets and track the evolving story...
